title: Apollo, Apollo

jokes.text: Are you frying bacon? No. It's my new running shoes.
jokes.setup: Jack hears strange rustling sound and guesses bacon
jokes.punchline: Reveals Liz's shoes are so loud they sound like bacon frying
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective misdirection. Perfectly efficient setup/payoff that establishes Liz's slovenly character.

jokes.text: My mail's been piling up, and I keep forgetting to buy toilet paper.
jokes.setup: Jack asks about not RSVPing to his party
jokes.punchline: Liz reveals she's using mail as toilet paper
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that's both disgusting and perfectly Liz. The casualness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Don't wear that thing with the belt. What thing with the belt? I have a lot of belted outfits!
jokes.setup: Jack gives vague fashion advice
jokes.punchline: Liz's panicked realization her entire wardrobe is awful
jokes.explanation: Good character beat about Liz's fashion sense with nice escalation from confusion to panic.

jokes.text: If you have a spaceship and are looking for a hilarious astronaut with an irregular heartbeat and $30 million
jokes.setup: Tracy announces he wants to go to space
jokes.punchline: Bizarre qualifications including medical problems and random cash amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity - the specific details like 'irregular heartbeat' make it funnier than generic weirdness.

jokes.text: Hello, dummy. No. Nope. Not interested. Have a good life.
jokes.setup: Dennis greets Liz at her door
jokes.punchline: Liz's immediate rejection before he can even speak
jokes.explanation: Efficient character interaction showing Liz's complete done-ness with Dennis. Great economy.

jokes.text: One word. Coffee. One problem. Where do you get it? Anywhere. You get it anywhere. Wrong.
jokes.setup: Dennis pitches his business idea
jokes.punchline: His solution to a non-existent problem
jokes.explanation: Great Dennis stupidity - the confident 'Wrong' to Liz's obvious correct answer is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: 38th and 6th in the basement of the K-Mart. You just go downstairs, you get the key from David, and boom. You plug in the machine
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his coffee vending machine business
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously complicated process for buying coffee
jokes.explanation: Dennis's business incompetence shown through absurdly inconvenient coffee access.

jokes.text: I am sorry that my disease has made you a victim of my sexual charisma. I am sorry that I have ruined you for other men.
jokes.setup: Dennis making amends as a 'sex addict'
jokes.punchline: His apology is actually bragging about his sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - Dennis's narcissism makes his 'apology' into self-aggrandizement. Perfect ironic structure.

jokes.text: My support group? It's lousy with nymphos.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining why he won't be tempted by hate sex
jokes.punchline: 'Lousy with' used for something desirable, revealing his true priorities
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay that reveals character - Dennis complaining about too many attractive women shows he's missing the point.

jokes.text: I have a Google News Alert for the phrase 'Tracy Jordan ridiculous disaster'.
jokes.setup: Liz asks if Jack has seen Tracy's space announcement
jokes.punchline: Jack's automated system for tracking Tracy's antics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack efficiency and Tracy chaos captured in one line. Very quotable and relatable to modern tech anxiety.

jokes.text: You think or you know? Hang on. I'm going into a tunnel.
jokes.setup: Jack pressing Pete for specifics about Tracy's schedule
jokes.punchline: Pete's obviously fake excuse to avoid giving real answer
jokes.explanation: Classic avoidance tactic, well-executed but not groundbreaking. Good character moment for Pete's conflict avoidance.

jokes.text: His name is Paul and he doesn't want to meet you.
jokes.setup: Jack reading his mother's birthday card
jokes.punchline: Brutal rejection delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical cruelty. The matter-of-fact delivery makes the rejection even funnier and more brutal.

jokes.text: As a kid, you never got so excited, you vomited? No. No one does that. I mean, I've peed a little.
jokes.setup: Jack defending childhood vomiting as normal
jokes.punchline: Liz's TMI admission about excitement urination
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Jack's weird thing to Liz's relatable but embarrassing admission. Classic Liz overshare.

jokes.text: I'm 'lizzing'! 'Lizzing' is a combination of 'laughing' and 'whizzing.'
jokes.setup: Liz coins a term for her accident
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau word for embarrassing bodily function
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that becomes a running gag. The term 'lizzing' is both disgusting and clever, perfectly Liz.

jokes.text: Kiss Peggy Fleming... Done, done and... oh, boy. Done.
jokes.setup: Reading childhood goals list
jokes.punchline: Jack's hesitation before confirming he kissed the figure skater
jokes.explanation: The pause and 'oh boy' suggest something inappropriate happened. Good character moment for Jack's past indiscretions.

jokes.text: Hit Mom with a car
jokes.setup: Reading more items from childhood list
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of wanting to hit mother with car
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy - treating matricidal intent as normal childhood goal. Excellent character insight into Jack's relationship with his mother.

jokes.text: I've held Walt Disney's frozen head in my hands.
jokes.setup: Jack comparing his adult achievements to childhood dreams
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of handling Disney's allegedly frozen head
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line - references conspiracy theory with complete casual confidence. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: I've hunted the world's most dangerous game. Man. Excuse me. Manatee.
jokes.setup: Jack claims to have hunted dangerous game
jokes.punchline: Correction from 'man' to harmless manatee
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - sets up expectation of human hunting, deflates to gentle sea cow. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: Could you get Adam West's agent on the phone, please?
jokes.setup: Jack wants to be friends with Batman
jokes.punchline: Calling the 1960s TV Batman actor instead of fictional character
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic - Jack's literal interpretation of wanting to be friends with Batman. Shows his wealth and connections.

jokes.text: Did you know there's a Broadway Street in Tampa?
jokes.setup: Agent represents Adam West who played Peter Pan
jokes.punchline: Random fact about Tampa geography
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor playing on Adam West's reputation for random observations. Not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Why are there so many dead doves up here?
jokes.setup: Jenna flying on theater rigging
jokes.punchline: Discovery of multiple dead birds in the fly space
jokes.explanation: Good dark visual gag about theater dangers. The casual delivery makes the gruesome discovery funnier.

jokes.text: Yes. Hello, this is Jenna.
jokes.setup: Liz answers Jenna's phone
jokes.punchline: Immediately pretends to be Jenna
jokes.explanation: Setup for the Dennis phone conversation. Basic deception comedy but necessary for the larger bit.

jokes.text: Yes. Really. Whysoever would you be phoning me?
jokes.setup: Liz impersonating Jenna
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top fake British accent and vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy about Liz's terrible impression skills. 'Whysoever' is a nice touch of fake sophistication.

jokes.text: With the help of Web M.D., I was recently self-diagnosed as a sex addict.
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining his addiction
jokes.punchline: Self-diagnosis via WebMD rather than real medical help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis incompetence - using internet for medical diagnosis. Relatable modern anxiety made character-specific.

jokes.text: You know, I was the love of Liz's life. I'm not sure you were, but apology accepted.
jokes.setup: Dennis boasting about his importance to Liz
jokes.punchline: Liz's deadpan dismissal while pretending to be Jenna
jokes.explanation: Great dramatic irony - Liz rejecting Dennis's claim about himself while he doesn't know it's her. Perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: What is this, Horseville? Because I am surrounded by 'neigh'-sayers. Wordplay.
jokes.setup: People are discouraging Tracy's space dreams
jokes.punchline: Horse pun followed by self-congratulatory meta-comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy meta-humor - making a pun then congratulating himself. The self-awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Of escaping the slums. Of killing an Ewok.
jokes.setup: Tracy listing his childhood space dreams
jokes.punchline: Escalation from noble goal to Star Wars violence
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from sympathetic backstory to random violence. The specificity of 'Ewok' makes it funnier than generic sci-fi reference.

jokes.text: Of course not. I don't have friends at NASA. Bunch of nerds.
jokes.setup: Jack promises to call NASA friends
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission he was lying and dismissive insult
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character moment. Jack's casual dismissal of NASA scientists fits his arrogant personality.

jokes.text: I lost my virginity to the My Fair Lady soundtrack.
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining her accent
jokes.punchline: TMI about sexual experience with Broadway music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna overshare that's both disgusting and perfectly in character. The specificity makes it memorable.

jokes.text: because of Hurricane Katrina
jokes.setup: Jenna explaining why she slept with Dennis
jokes.punchline: Using national tragedy as excuse for bad decisions
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about using tragedy as excuse. Perfectly captures Jenna's self-centered worldview.

jokes.text: You were trying to be an actress then, despite your neck.
jokes.setup: Jenna reminiscing about meeting Liz
jokes.punchline: Casual insult about Liz's physical appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna casual cruelty. The specificity of criticizing someone's neck is absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Are you thinking what I'm thinking? Yeah. Let's go give him a piece of our mind. Let's stab him. Yeah, your thing.
jokes.setup: Friends planning to confront Dennis together
jokes.punchline: Jenna suggests murder, Liz deflects back to her milder plan
jokes.explanation: Great escalation comedy. Jenna's casual suggestion of murder followed by Liz's diplomatic redirect is perfect character work.

jokes.text: But I thought you were the oldest, sir. Oldest boy.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions wearing hand-me-downs
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had older sisters who gave him their clothes
jokes.explanation: Efficient misdirection revealing Jack's feminine upbringing. Good economy and character insight.

jokes.text: Every time you move his head, his head moves! Look!
jokes.setup: Kenneth showing off his keychain gift
jokes.punchline: Explaining obvious cause-and-effect as if it's magical
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth childlike wonder. The redundancy of explaining that moving a head makes it move is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: Kenneth, I wonder what it's like seeing the world through your eyes. I don't know, Mr. Donaghy. I think I see the world pretty much the same as everyone else.
jokes.setup: Jack wondering about Kenneth's unique perspective
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's complete lack of self-awareness about his weirdness
jokes.explanation: Great ironic moment - Kenneth has no idea how weird he is. Sets up the musical sequence that follows.

jokes.text: Musical sequence about Kenneth's simple joy
jokes.setup: Jack's philosophical moment about Kenneth
jokes.punchline: Breaks into sudden musical number
jokes.explanation: Absurdist musical break that parodies TV movie moments. More of a visual/audio gag than dialogue comedy.

jokes.text: You've got all of these fancy presents from business associates and this big empty house to store them in.
jokes.setup: Kenneth commenting on Jack's birthday happiness
jokes.punchline: Inadvertent observation about Jack's lonely wealthy life
jokes.explanation: Kenneth's innocent observation accidentally reveals Jack's emotional emptiness. Good dark comedy disguised as simple comment.

jokes.text: You want me to rank you. Apples and oranges, ladies.
jokes.setup: Liz and Jenna confront Dennis
jokes.punchline: Dennis assumes they want sexual comparison
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis narcissism - completely misreading the situation. His confidence in his sexual prowess is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What those people were doing to the Superdome?
jokes.setup: Jenna justifying being upset about Hurricane Katrina
jokes.punchline: Focuses on damage to building rather than human suffering
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about Jenna's misplaced priorities. Shows her superficial understanding of serious events.

jokes.text: It was in your bed. Aw, guys, come on! I eat in there!
jokes.setup: Revelation about where Dennis and Jenna had sex
jokes.punchline: Liz's disgusted reaction about eating in her defiled bed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and perfect Liz reaction. The detail about eating in bed makes it more gross and character-specific.

jokes.text: I'm ranking you now. Hey, blondie. You're number one. I don't care! Thank you, Dennis.
jokes.setup: Dennis deciding to rank the women sexually
jokes.punchline: Jenna's contradictory response - claiming not to care while thanking him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna vanity - can't help being pleased even when claiming indifference. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I can't stay long. I'm on a case. The Penguin's in town.
jokes.setup: Adam West arrives at Jack's party
jokes.punchline: Still thinks he's Batman fighting real villains
jokes.explanation: Classic Adam West delusion. Perfect callback to Jack wanting to be friends with Batman, but getting confused actor.

jokes.text: Jake 'Dellahee'!
jokes.setup: Adam West introducing Jack at his party
jokes.punchline: Completely mispronounces 'Donaghy'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation gag. Shows Adam West's confusion and adds to Jack's birthday disappointment.

jokes.text: I was told I'd get a meal.
jokes.setup: Jack cancels his party
jokes.punchline: Adam West's only concern is missing dinner
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan moment. Adam West's practical concern amid Jack's emotional crisis is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: The closest I came to vomiting tonight was when I saw Ann Coulter's shoulder blades.
jokes.setup: Jack comparing his adult disappointment to childhood joy
jokes.punchline: Specific gross-out detail about conservative pundit's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Great specific visual joke. The random targeting of Ann Coulter's shoulder blades is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: I'm going to Benjamin Button myself.
jokes.setup: Jack planning to recapture childhood happiness
jokes.punchline: Reference to aging backwards movie as actual plan
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference used absurdly. Jack's wealth makes this seem almost plausible for him.

jokes.text: As a child, I dreamed of being a Congressman like my father. Now, I do this. One D.U.I. in high school.
jokes.setup: Pete explaining how he ended up in TV
jokes.punchline: Single drunk driving incident ruined political career dreams
jokes.explanation: Good dark character backstory. Shows how small mistakes can derail entire life plans, very Pete-like.

jokes.text: Computer, when do I get some Tang? Also... I'm thirsty.
jokes.setup: Tracy asking space computer about Tang drink
jokes.punchline: Realizes he made an unintended sexual pun
jokes.explanation: Simple double entendre with Tracy's self-awareness. Not groundbreaking but fits character.

jokes.text: I threw a brick through the window of a Banana Republic.
jokes.setup: Jenna feeling empowered after confronting Dennis
jokes.punchline: Channeled aggression into random property damage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Jenna's misdirected energy. The random target makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Floor Marshall! / Hey. You called tech support?
jokes.setup: Liz calls for emergency Floor Marshall after Jenna's accident
jokes.punchline: Tech support guy shows up instead, missing the emergency
jokes.explanation: Mild workplace confusion gag - wrong department responding to emergency

jokes.text: I flew down from Boston. / I have to know, Jimmy. / I don't remember, Jack.
jokes.setup: Jack desperately questioning Jimmy about the gift
jokes.punchline: Jimmy doesn't remember and just wants the job
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Jack's obsession versus Jimmy's practical concerns about employment.

jokes.text: If I learned anything from watching 24, you're going to want to zoom in and enhance.
jokes.setup: Jack needs to analyze old video
jokes.punchline: Reference to TV trope about impossible video enhancement
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about TV technology tropes. Minor character but relatable reference.

jokes.text: I thought this was a job interview. I flew down from Boston.
jokes.setup: Jack interrogating childhood friend about birthday gift
jokes.punchline: Jimmy's confusion about why he's really there
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment about Jack's wealth allowing him to fly people in for trivial reasons.

jokes.text: You're clearly saying something. Get me a deaf person.
jokes.setup: Can't hear what young Jack is saying in video
jokes.punchline: Wants to hire lip reader rather than find volume
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jack logic - using wealth and power for simple problem. The specificity makes it funnier than generic rich guy joke.

jokes.text: Why wouldn't you warn me? I was staring at your mouth!
jokes.setup: Deaf lip reader watches young Jack vomit
jokes.punchline: Her disgust at not being warned about the vomiting
jokes.explanation: Good callback payoff. The lip reader's specific complaint about watching the mouth makes it funnier.

jokes.text: These crutches are real, everyone. Liz tried to kill me.
jokes.setup: Jenna on crutches after Liz's fall
jokes.punchline: Dramatic accusation of attempted murder
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna drama escalation. Her interpretation of accident as murder attempt is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Are go for launch. Star Wars. [Imitates Ewok]
jokes.setup: Tracy preparing for fake space launch
jokes.punchline: References Star Wars and makes Ewok sounds, connecting to earlier Ewok murder comment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Tracy's earlier Star Wars reference - consistent character obsession

jokes.text: I'm scared! But I'm also excited! I'm 'lizzing'!
jokes.setup: Tracy experiencing fake space launch
jokes.punchline: Uses Liz's made-up word for his own experience
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows the word spreading. Tracy using it makes perfect sense for his character.

jokes.text: where a morbidly obese gentleman called me 'pal.'
jokes.setup: Jack bought expensive Apollo toy
jokes.punchline: His disgust at being treated casually by store clerk
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack snobbery. His horror at informal address shows his class consciousness and entitlement.

jokes.text: And sometimes, it sounds like Jenna yelling in the distance.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing space experience
jokes.punchline: Can hear Jenna from the studio next door
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the fake setup. Tracy's casual mention reveals the illusion while he stays in character.

jokes.text: If everyone could see the world the way I see it... it would be a better place to live.
jokes.setup: Tracy philosophizing about perspective
jokes.punchline: Claiming his insane worldview would improve everything
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment - Tracy's complete lack of self-awareness about his chaotic influence on the world.

jokes.text: the only job she ever booked was for a local phone sex line
jokes.setup: Jenna revealing Liz's commercial
jokes.punchline: It was for a phone sex service
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Liz's embarrassment. Sets up the video revelation for bigger comedy payoff.

jokes.text: they only hired her because their first choice was deported
jokes.setup: Explaining how Liz got the phone sex job
jokes.punchline: She was a replacement for deported actress
jokes.explanation: Good escalation making Liz's 'achievement' even more pathetic. The deportation detail is specific and mean.

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Bijou. Call me.
jokes.setup: Watching Liz's old commercial
jokes.punchline: Seeing awkward young Liz in phone sex ad
jokes.explanation: Great visual payoff to the setup. The contrast between current Liz and 'Bijou' is perfectly cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: I have since had 'Invisalign'.
jokes.setup: Everyone seeing young Liz's bad teeth
jokes.punchline: Liz's defensive explanation of dental improvement
jokes.explanation: Good defensive reaction. Liz's specific mention of Invisalign shows her continued insecurity about appearance.

jokes.text: That's not even enough numbers!
jokes.setup: Phone sex commercial with phone number
jokes.punchline: Number shown doesn't have enough digits to work
jokes.explanation: Good observational detail about the commercial's cheapness. Shows the production values of Liz's acting career.

jokes.text: I remember that girl. She cried all day.
jokes.setup: Kenneth recognizing young Liz from commercial
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of her emotional breakdown during shoot
jokes.explanation: Good Kenneth innocence revealing embarrassing detail. Makes Liz's humiliation even worse.

jokes.text: Oh, is that Billie Jean King?
jokes.setup: Kenneth seeing Liz's phone sex commercial for first time
jokes.punchline: Mistakes Liz for famous tennis player
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth innocence - completely missing the sexual context

jokes.text: Bijou.
jokes.setup: Jack saying goodbye to Liz
jokes.punchline: Calls her by her phone sex persona name
jokes.explanation: Perfect final callback. One word that encapsulates Jack's amusement and Liz's eternal embarrassment. Great economy.

tmdb_overview: While Jack puts together the perfect 50th birthday party for himself, home videos inspire him to try to recreate his happiest moment. Dennis decides to tell Liz about his addiction. Meanwhile, Kenneth and Pete work to make Tracy's childhood dream come true.
insight: 69 jokes in 23 minutes: 30 Rock's densest episode channels absurdist chaos over setup.
